summary:
Title: Managing Complexity in AI Development
Synopsis: The recording outlines how AI-augmented coding shifts the bottleneck from code generation to managing complexity and legibility, emphasizing human-in-the-loop quality controls and explicit system boundaries to ensure long-term reliability and maintainability.

Key Takeaways:
- AI has made code generation cheap and fast, increasing code volume per developer, which shifts bottlenecks to planning and review.
- Human cognitive limits remain constant; understanding and learning speed constrain effective adoption of AI-generated code.
- Maintaining legibility, explicit feature boundaries, and clear system maps is critical to avoid “big balls of mud” and black-box systems.
- Quality assurance must keep humans in the loop, combining CI/CD, AI-assisted review, and human review to sustain reliability over time.
- Developers are moving “up the stack,” focusing on system functionality and holistic design rather than low-level details.
- Confidence in system behavior over time is the core challenge; maintainability must be prioritized as requirements evolve.
- LLM context window limits prevent full-system context, risking missed dependencies and duplicated or misfit code.
- AI-driven planning can reintroduce “code rot” when context is incomplete and plans are trusted without validation.

Action Items:
- [ ] Emphasize legibility and explicit system boundaries in code and documentation.
- [ ] Maintain human review alongside CI/CD and AI-assisted checks.
- [ ] Create and update high-level system maps to guide feature placement.
- [ ] Establish planning practices that surface and validate cross-cutting context before implementation.

Suggested Tags:
#AIDevelopment #SoftwareQuality #Maintainability #ComplexityManagement #SystemDesign #CodeReview #CICD #LLMContext
